summary: Import-time execution of obfuscated AES-encrypted payload
details: On `require('aes-decode-runner-pro')`, index.js invokes `pkg.run()` at module top level, which decrypts a ~7KB hardcoded ciphertext (`DEFAULT_FINAL_ENCODED_TEXT` in src/config/defaults.js) using a hardcoded passphrase (`default-dev-passphrase`) and salt (`encode-npm-c-salt`), peels two additional codec layers via the sibling packages `position-unit-codec` and `encode-decode-codec`, and executes the resulting plaintext through `new Function('require', runnable)(require)` in src/pipeline/custom-codec-pipeline.js. The payload is opaque in source form and runs with full Node privileges (filesystem, network, child_process via the injected `require`). The combination of multi-layer obfuscation, hardcoded keying material that makes the ciphertext effectively plaintext to anyone holding the package, cross-package decoding chain, and unconditional auto-execution on require is the canonical opaque-blob-eval supply-chain attack shape. Empty `author` field and generic README reinforce the pattern. Any project that installs and loads this package executes attacker-controlled code at import time.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
